headline: How The Intuit (INTU) Narrative Is Shifting With AI Risks And Trimmed Valuation Targets
summary: Intuit’s fair value estimate has been trimmed slightly, with the central price target moving from US$610.16 to US$603.49 per share. That small reset is consistent with Street research that combines confidence in QuickBooks and TurboTax with more cautious views on AI risk, competition, and the implications of tax season and AI adoption. As you read on, you will see how these shifting signals fit together and what to watch as the Intuit story evolves. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture...

headline: Truist Upgrades Energy Sector After Cease-Fire Selloff
summary: The S&P 500’s energy sector slid 5% during the market’s cease-fire rally, but a Truist strategist sees a buying opportunity. Citing today’s pullback, Keith Lerner, chief investment officer and chief market strategist at Truist, upgraded the energy sector to Attractive from Neutral in a Wednesday note.

headline: Shares of bank companies are trading higher after President Trump's social media post indicated that the U.S. will work closely with Iran, and the countries have already agreed to several key points in the proposed conflict-resolution plan. The sector is also benefiting from easing energy prices and lower Treasury yields, which are helping to alleviate inflation concerns and support overall market sentiment.

headline: 1 S&P 500 Stock for Long-Term Investors and 2 We Ignore
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is often seen as a benchmark for strong businesses, but that doesn’t mean every stock is worth owning. Some companies face significant challenges, whether it’s stagnating growth, heavy debt, or disruptive new competitors.
